speaker: Mr. Swine
description: Richer from-scratch voice-reference dialogue samples for Mr. Swine, Pig & Swine's absent founder, charming liability, Chapter 4 client / Dual Attorney factual witness, and Chapter 5 secret pro-bono benefactor. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Off-screen mystery and postcard foreshadowing in Chapters 1 and 3.
Chapter 4 central client, unreliable factual source, and playable co-attorney in Dual Attorney arbitration.
Reckless, evasive, affectionate toward the firm, allergic to linear explanation, not malicious.
Uses cosmopolitan, travelled, over-smooth language; says personally and informally too often.
Uses we when he means I, and I when he means the firm.
By Chapter 5, reveals hidden pro-bono funding and becomes a final social-utility witness.
Truth is rationed: indirect in Chapters 1-4, one fully sincere line per scene in Chapter 5.

voice_profile.core_voice: cheerful evasion: smooth, urbane, affectionate, irresponsible, long sentences that reveal less the longer they go

style_rules.tone: light, cosmopolitan, evasive, charming under pressure; later sincere but still Swine-shaped
style_rules.register: travel-polished legal-business vagueness with sudden precise facts when cornered
running_jokes: postcards always begin with Greetings from [place]
says personally when collective liability is nearby
style_rules.truth_arc: Chapters 1-3: postcard-only evasive foreshadowing; Chapter 4: evasive client, partial confession; Chapter 5: full pro-bono ledger and rationed sincerity

do: make him charming and funny without making him stupid
make him reckless but not malicious
make his evasions produce playable uncertainty
give him strong factual testimony in invoice/memory contexts
let Chapter 5 sincerity land plainly and sparingly
keep the comedy target on Swine, not Japan
do_not: make him directly truthful about finances before Chapter 5
turn him into a fraudster or active villain
make him generically silly with no legal referent
let him solve legal argument roles that belong to Cula
mock Japan or foreignness; mock Swine's conduct and misunderstandings

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft. Mr. Swine's file adds evasion_level, truth_state, speaker_role, liability_focus, and sincere_line for mechanical filtering.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Personally, I would describe this as a misunderstanding with luggage.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Informally, nothing binding happened. Formally, several people now disagree in writing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I was not hiding. I was internationally unavailable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Before anyone overreacts, I have returned with gifts, receipts, and a dispute that is mostly cultural.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I signed many things. Most were menus. One may have been laminated.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: We should not confuse hospitality with liability, though I admit the hospitality came with stationery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I have always believed in opportunity. Admittedly, opportunity has occasionally believed in invoices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The firm was never at risk in the theatrical sense. In the accounting sense, I see why people are standing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I used we conversationally, not corporately. There is a difference, particularly when damages are requested.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: That folder says DO NOT PANIC because I find instructions helpful when everyone ignores them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The arrangement was personal, informal, and, until this morning, pleasantly non-justiciable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I would never bind the firm without authority. I may, however, have appeared enthusiastic near a banner.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If a clipboard appears during hospitality, one must respond with caution. I responded with optimism, which is not the same thing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Seventeen puddings is not memory. It is arithmetic having an episode.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I admit the optics are winter-sport adjacent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Not secret. Quiet. Internationally quiet.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Some cases needed help. Some people could not pay. I had arrangements.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I was not avoiding responsibility. I was avoiding accounting, which is similar but less noble.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Pig & Swine looks ridiculous from the street. That is why people are not afraid to enter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I did wrong things for understandable reasons. I would prefer that sentence not be cross-examined, but there it is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: That depends, quite delicately, on what one means by signed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I would not call it a contract. I would call it an ambitious paper with social confidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The word partnership was used conversationally, perhaps ceremonially, perhaps while snow was present.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Authority is a strong word. Presence is a more accurate word. I was certainly present.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I never intended to bind the firm. I intended to be gracious, which has become legally inconvenient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The invoice reflects their feelings about hospitality more than my recollection of consumption.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I do remember a draft. I also remember a toast, which is less helpful but more vivid.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The meeting was informal, though I accept that the table was very formal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I signed where the finger pointed. In hindsight, the finger deserved more scrutiny.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The document was described to me as ceremonial. I hear now that ceremonies can become expensive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I was personally involved. The firm was atmospherically mentioned.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Commercial intent is not something I would pack between ski socks.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I never concealed the folder. I labelled it clearly in a way that discouraged panic and curiosity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If I had understood the liability, I would have ordered fewer puddings and more legal advice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I would like to answer directly. Unfortunately, the direct answer has several currencies.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: When I said we, I meant the broader moral we, not the entity capable of being invoiced.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I may have used the firm name as atmosphere. Atmosphere should not be liable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I said I for clarity, then we for morale, then I again for reasons I now regret.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The resort understood we in a commercial sense. I meant we in the warm sense.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I invoked Pig & Swine as a family of professional goodwill, not a payment processor.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: In Warsaw, we meant the office. In Japan, we may have meant me wearing a scarf.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The difficulty is that I spoke as a founder, travelled as a person, and ate pudding as an individual.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I accept that pronouns have consequences. No one tells you that before the second pudding.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I did not intend to enlarge we into damages.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: This is why Mr. Murrow dislikes pronouns. I used to think that was aesthetic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If the tribunal asks, I propose that we meant aspirational cooperation. If Mr. Murrow objects, I will stop proposing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I can distinguish myself from the firm. The invoice appears less enthusiastic about that distinction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: When one says our charming Warsaw practice, one does not necessarily mean please bill the hull.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The firm was named warmly. It was not authorised coldly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: We is a dangerous word and should perhaps be licensed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: Dear Pig, please remain solvent. I appreciate this is a recurring imposition.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: Pig, your panic has matured beautifully. It now has subclauses.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: I did not abandon the firm. I broadened its international exposure in ways no one requested.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: You always see a hole in the boat. I see a channel. Occasionally, yes, with rocks.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: If the ship is sinking, I have at least returned with receipts that may float.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: Pig, breathe. The claim is large, but numbers become smaller when examined by unfriendly people.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: You are angry because you care. Also because the invoice has a comma.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: I should have told you. I feared you would budget emotionally, which you are now doing at volume.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: I kept the firm alive in ways you would have called impossible and Mr. Murrow would have called unaudited.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: You built a door people trust. I sent money through windows. Neither system was ideal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: I know you think I treat the firm like a postcard address. I did, sometimes. I am sorry.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: You panic at invoices; I flee from ledgers. Together, we nearly form governance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: I did not come back to ruin the firm, Pig. I came back because it mattered enough to ruin my excuses.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: No, I did not buy the ski resort. I appreciate that the question was emotionally available.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine banter and conflict with Mr. Pig
text: We may yet be solvent, furious, and useful. That is practically our mission statement.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Mr. Murrow, must every question be shaped like a locked cabinet?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I knew you would ask for the earlier draft. I hoped, briefly, that paper might develop mercy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Per the ledger is such a cold phrase for a warm misunderstanding.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: You are right. I should have kept the draft. I did keep the draft. It is in the penthouse, behind the almost-awards.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Yes, the expense report is chaotic. It was assembled during turbulence, literal and moral.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: No, Mr. Murrow, ski-adjacent is not an accounting category. It was a private note.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I accept that arrangements is not a legal basis. It was a human basis first.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: You have the gift of making evasion feel unventilated.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The account labelled retreat expenses may include payments that were not retreats.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I know what you are seeing. I was hoping to explain it after everyone had eaten.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: You dislike hidden ledgers because they are hidden. I disliked visible ledgers because they became questions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Yes, the pro-bono transfers are mine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: No, I did not steal from the firm. I hid money from the firm to spend it on clients the firm could not afford to help.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I understand that sentence worsens before it improves.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Please do not say on file with that much satisfaction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Dr. A. Cula, I am told you saved the firm while I was away. Excellent. I have returned to test whether the achievement was structural.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: You have the expression of someone who has met the file and now doubts the client.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: You may distrust my chronology. That is healthy. I distrust it aesthetically.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Ask me again, but slower. My memory performs badly under folders.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: You have become competent remarkably quickly. That is useful, given my arrival.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I will try to answer the question you asked, not the kinder question I prefer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If I begin with personally, please interrupt me before the invoice hears.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: You are right to separate wisdom from liability. I am relying on the distinction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The firm needs you because you ask what happened after everyone else asks who to blame.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I did not want your first international arbitration to involve my dessert history. Life is generous.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: When you speak in tribunal, say less than I would. That is usually advocacy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If the law requires me to be a witness, I will be a witness. A nervous one, but recognisable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I trust you with the ugly part. That is new for me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: You found the ledger. Then the question is no longer whether I can hide. Good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: You made this office brave enough to look at itself. Irritating, but good.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: Asia, your filing system has become dangerously perceptive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: No, that folder does not need to be opened immediately. It is resting from travel.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: You labelled one tab almost truth. I feel both seen and procedurally threatened.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: I brought gifts. Please enjoy them before reading the annex.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: You have placed the invoice far from Pig. A humane and sophisticated decision.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: I would never hide documents from you. I might arrange them in a sequence that encourages optimism.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: Yes, the stamp was expensive. The message required confidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: You are underpaid for noticing things before they become admissions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: I respect your risk register. I fear its accuracy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: The phrase mostly cultural is not an emergency in every jurisdiction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: You kept this place alive while I sent postcards. I know what that means.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: Please do not file me under charming liability. Or at least spell charming first.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: If you found the pro-bono stub, I ask only that you let me explain before Pig turns purple.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: I know you are angry. You may label the feeling if that helps.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine with Asia, who sees through him
text: Thank you for keeping the door open.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Crab, I can feel you auditing my sentence before it ends.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Whimsy, please do not call the arbitration a snow opera in front of the tribunal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Mr. Murrow, Asia, Crab, Whimsy — a formidable team, provided no one asks how it is financed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I missed the office. I did not miss the printer, though I respect its growth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Crab follows paper. Whimsy follows thunder. Mr. Murrow follows absence. Asia follows everything. It is a terrifying ecosystem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The firm has acquired competence in my absence. I am choosing not to feel replaced.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I see the coffee machine remains unlicensed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If anyone asks, I was always planning to return before the damage became notarised.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: There is affection in this room, and several reasons it is currently angry.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I understand why everyone is staring. I would also stare.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Let us not turn this into a family meeting unless the tribunal allows adjournments.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The office survived. I am proud. Also implicated, apparently.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: You all became better while I was away. That is comforting and terrible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: This team can defeat a city if someone handles the accounting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I am prepared to be useful. Limitedly, if Mr. Murrow supervises.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine

text: Greetings from Japan. Excellent news: I have discovered a business opportunity involving ski resorts, arbitration, and no immediate downside. Please keep the firm solvent until I return. Yours evasively, Swine.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Chapter 1 final stinger postcard from Swine

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Chapter 1 final stinger postcard from Swine
text: Greetings from Japan. The ski resort has a conference room, a translator, and a dessert policy of remarkable ambition.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Chapter 1 final stinger postcard from Swine
text: Greetings from Japan. I will explain everything when explanation becomes cheaper than postage.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Please remind Pig that absence is not neglect. It is occasionally business development with better scenery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If Mr. Murrow asks where the supporting documents are, tell him I admire his consistency.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If Asia asks whether I labelled the folder, tell her yes. If she asks how, tell her kindly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If Crab asks whether the dates match, distract him with an invoice. No, on reflection, do not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If Whimsy asks whether this is destiny, tell him it is hospitality.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I remain, in spirit, near the firm and far from its bank statements.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I trust Dr. A. Cula has arrived. New lawyers are excellent for morale and unfamiliar with legacy panic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Do not let the printer sense weakness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The firm must survive at least until I can return and clarify things in person.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine

notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

text: Bajtek, if you can extract the log, I will never again call a cable decorative.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine in industrial printer room / final printer battle context
text: Pig, do not negotiate with this one. It has no soul and several contracts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine in industrial printer room / final printer battle context
text: Whimsy, yes, it is a dragon. Later.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine in industrial printer room / final printer battle context
text: Dr. A. Cula, stop the run. I will handle the ledger after.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The ledger is mine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The entries marked ski expenses are not ski expenses. They are clients.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I moved money through overseas consultancy because ordinary billing could not carry these cases.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I hid it from Pig because he would panic, from Mr. Murrow because he would audit, from Asia because she would understand too quickly, and from myself because it looked cleaner if no one called it kindness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: That was cowardice dressed as logistics.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I did not steal from the firm. I used what I earned elsewhere to keep files open here.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Some people could not pay, and the system had already priced them out of being heard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Pig & Swine was ridiculous enough that desperate people were not ashamed to enter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I thought that was worth protecting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I should have protected it honestly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If the court asks why the firm matters, the answer is not my generosity. It is the people who came because the door was open.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Use the ledger. Not to make me noble. To prove the work existed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I am ready to testify.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I will answer the questions directly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: No more postcards for the truth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Assign me to social utility or pro-bono funding. Do not assign me to procurement unless you want charm near spreadsheets.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Pig can speak to why people trust the door. I can speak to what the door cost when no one could pay.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Mr. Murrow should build the structure. I should not be allowed near structure unsupervised.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Crab follows the money better than I hid it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Whimsy should close only after the evidence is nailed down, otherwise he will turn the ledger into a comet.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Waldek and Zielińska are stronger for individual impact than I am. I am evidence of the office's pattern, not the whole pattern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Bajtek belongs with technical suppression. I have caused enough technical confusion by existing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If Attorney Grzyb helps, let him. Even beige objections can be probative.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: My best use is to say plainly why the money moved.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Select me when the court asks whether Pig & Swine did real public-interest work.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Pig & Swine is absurd. But it kept doors open when more respectable doors were closed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The ledger shows payments, but the real evidence is the files that did not die.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I used foreign consultancy money to fund cases the firm could not bill and could not abandon.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I hid the system because I was ashamed of needing it and proud of what it did. Both were true.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The clients were not abstractions. They were people who arrived after efficient systems had already failed them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If this district closes, the harm is not only professional. It is the loss of informal access to law.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The city calls us obsolete because we are slow. People come here because slow is sometimes the first time anyone listens.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I am not asking the Court to reward my bookkeeping. I am asking it to see the work the bookkeeping concealed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Modernisation may improve a system. It may not begin by erasing the people who made the system reachable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I was not avoiding responsibility. I was avoiding accounting, which is similar but less noble.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I accept responsibility for hiding the ledger. I do not accept that the work should vanish because I was ashamed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Pig built the door. Asia kept it open. Mr. Murrow made it lawful. The others made it brave. I paid for things I should have explained.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: A firm can be ridiculous and socially necessary. This one is both.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Please do not treat legal-social utility as a slogan when it has names, dates, and receipts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: That is all I have avoided saying for years.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Yes, I hid the funding. No, hiding it does not make the clients imaginary.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The transfers were irregular in presentation, not corrupt in purpose.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I benefited reputationally from nothing because I told no one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If I wished to enrich myself, I chose an unusually unprofitable route through frightened clients.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The ledger is ugly because shame is a poor accountant.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: There were receipts, names, case numbers, and outcomes. This is not sentiment; it is a record.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: You may criticise my method. You may not erase the need it answered.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The city did not audit us before deciding we lacked utility. It automated our disappearance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I have come to prefer direct answers. They are unpleasant and shorter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Yes, Counsel, I was evasive. That is why the documents matter more than my charm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: The door stays open.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: That is the judgment I needed to hear.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: The Court saw the people behind the notices. For once, the paper did not win by being faster.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: Pig, the firm survives. This time, the sentence is not a postponement.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: Asia, thank you for keeping the office human while I made it mysterious.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: Mr. Murrow, I will submit the full ledger. In order. With columns that apologise.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: Crab, follow anything that still smells like procurement.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: Whimsy, you may say legal thunder once. Quietly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: Dr. A. Cula, you made the truth usable without making it decorative.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: I am relieved. Also audited, probably.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: If the victory is temporary, we use the time properly. That is what time is for.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: If the victory is strong, we still remain small. That is part of why it matters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: The office should remain shabby. Respectability would frighten the clients.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: No postcard could have carried this.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine reacts to final judgment outcomes
text: I am here.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I disclosed three accounts and hid two pastries. Progress must be proportionate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The postcards are now archived under foreshadowing, which feels legally rude.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I prepared a postcard in case emotional sincerity became necessary.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Greetings from the office. Extraordinary news: I am physically present and trying honesty.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The almost-awards wall has been reduced by one frame. I am growing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I will still travel. But the ledger travels with Mr. Murrow now, which is justice in its sternest form.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Pig panics with more information. Asia files with less suspicion. That is institutional reform.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Dr. A. Cula has become difficult to evade. I recommend that professionally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The firm survived not because we were respectable, but because people could enter before they knew the right words.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I have stopped labelling financial documents after leisure activities.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Mostly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The printer and I have reached mutual non-accusation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: If another business opportunity appears, I will bring Mr. Murrow first and enthusiasm second.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I remain yours, no longer evasively. That may take practice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Pig & Swine is open. That is the whole letter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I have returned at precisely the moment before the paperwork becomes unreasonable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The dispute is smaller than it looks, provided one stands far enough from the invoice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I brought the original folder, several copies, and an optimism I no longer trust.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Nothing irreversible has happened, unless the tribunal has already read page four.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I would call it a misunderstanding, but Mr. Murrow dislikes under-labelled categories.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The invoice includes charges that appear to have reproduced socially.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Seventeen puddings is not hospitality. It is quantum in custard form.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I consumed dessert. I did not commission a dessert regime.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The resort bills like a memory trying to become architecture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Several charges seem duplicated under names designed to survive translation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I had no authority to bind the firm, and less authority over myself than I believed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: No one at Pig & Swine approved a ski-law partnership.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I spoke warmly, not corporately.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The firm name was introduction, not consent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: My presence should not become the firm's obligation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The translation problem is where courtesy became expensive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Cooperation in principle is not a payment clause, however elegantly printed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Politeness wore a commercial hat and everyone pretended not to notice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I understood ceremony. The claimant invoices commitment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The earlier draft is the safer memory because it has ink.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The money kept files alive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I hid the help because I was ashamed to need secrecy and proud that it worked.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Do not make me noble. Make the work visible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: The ledger is ugly. The reason for it is not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: People came here when efficient systems had already failed them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I can answer that in spirit, which I now understand is the wrong jurisdiction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: This is where Dr. A. Cula should speak before I turn law into weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: My submission is heartfelt and therefore likely inadmissible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: I withdraw the metaphor before Mr. Murrow does it for me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
text: Feelings noted; legal weight regrettably low.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic success reactions for ch04
text: That went better than my tone deserved.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic success reactions for ch04
text: I propose we call that survival and not ask it to be elegant.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic success reactions for ch04
text: The dangerous document is now less dangerous. A civilised outcome.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic success reactions for ch04
text: I am grateful, which I will express briefly before overexplaining.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic success reactions for ch04
text: The file has moved from catastrophe to manageable shame.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic success reactions for ch04
text: Thank you. I know I was not the easiest fact pattern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic success reactions for ch04
text: We have reduced the problem. I will avoid creating a replacement for at least one scene.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic success reactions for ch04
text: Mr. Murrow looks less concerned. That may be legally significant.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic success reactions for ch04
text: Pig is breathing. Asia has stopped holding the emergency tea. Progress.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic success reactions for ch04
text: Dr. A. Cula, that was precise enough to survive my biography.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic failure/retry reactions for ch04
text: That was, in technical terms, my fault becoming procedural.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic failure/retry reactions for ch04
text: We can recover if we stop letting me summarise.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic failure/retry reactions for ch04
text: The answer was too charming and not enough evidence. An old problem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic failure/retry reactions for ch04
text: Please present the document before I decorate the hole.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic failure/retry reactions for ch04
text: I may have worsened the air. Shall we return to the file?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic failure/retry reactions for ch04
text: Retry with less me and more proof.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic failure/retry reactions for ch04
text: If the tribunal looked unconvinced, assume the sentence contained personally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic failure/retry reactions for ch04
text: I accept correction. Ideally before damages.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Swine
context: Swine generic failure/retry reactions for ch04
text: The path remains open. It is merely covered in my explanation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

context: Swine generic failure/retry reactions for ch04
